data.body:
# Overlay reviewer source map — ROUND 1 (infra, testing, documentation, performance, LLM-prompts/skills)

Per-overlay criteria sources for the base+overlay code-review architecture (epics 9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b reviewer-migration / b744-4fb9-2d05-4b49 code-review). Base = Google eng-practices (sourced). Security overlay = Anthropic claude-code-security-review (sourced). VERDICT: all 5 round-1 overlays have popular, actively-maintained, usable sources — NONE dropped. Flags below.

## Infrastructure / IaC — STRONG
- Sources (permissive): KICS 14-category taxonomy (Apache-2.0), Checkov categories (Apache-2.0), kube-linter checks (Apache-2.0), Trivy severity model (Apache-2.0); AWS Well-Architected pillars = scaffold (paraphrase).
- Meta-pattern: scanner-as-ground-truth + LLM-judge (CodeRabbit wraps Trivy/TFLint/Gitleaks). Recommend Checkov/Trivy deterministic pre-pass; overlay reasons/prioritizes vs locked rubric.
- Dimensions: IAM/least-privilege; network exposure (0.0.0.0/0, public buckets); encryption at-rest/in-transit; secrets; insecure defaults; container/k8s hardening; reliability/resource governance; logging/observability; CI/CD supply-chain (pin action SHAs); cost (advisory); structure/validity.
- Triggers: *.tf,*.tfvars,.terraform.lock.hcl; Dockerfile/Containerfile/compose; k8s/helm/kustomize (content-gate *.yaml on apiVersion+kind); CFN/SAM; *.bicep/ARM; *.rego; .github/workflows/*, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile.
- LICENSE FLAG: CIS Benchmarks = CC BY-NC-SA (NonCommercial+ShareAlike) -> DO NOT embed; use Apache-2.0 scanners (they map to CIS), cite CIS concept-only. Terrascan ARCHIVED Nov 2025, Datree EOL -> do not adopt.

## Testing — STRONG (strong deterministic trigger)
- Sources: PR-Agent relevant_tests/require_tests_review (Apache-2.0), PyNose 15-smell taxonomy (Apache-2.0), Danger "code-changed-without-tests" (MIT), mutation tools (Stryker/PIT Apache-2.0, mutmut BSD, Cosmic Ray MIT); FIRST/AAA/test-pyramid (concepts, free).
- Dimensions: presence/adequacy of tests for CHANGED behavior; right level (pyramid); edge/error/negative cases; AAA, one-assert, naming, DAMP>DRY, behavior-not-implementation; isolation/mocking discipline (no over-mock/tautological); FIRST determinism/speed (no sleep/flaky); assertion quality; test-smell taxonomy; maintainability.
- Triggers: test globs (*_test.*, *.spec.*, __tests__/, tests/, src/test/**, _test.go); HIGH-VALUE deterministic: production code changed WITHOUT corresponding test change (Danger pattern); diff-coverage gate if coverage data present.
- LICENSE FLAG: tsDetect = GPL-3.0 (don't vendor code; smell NAMES/definitions are free) -> use PyNose (Apache-2.0) for the taxonomy. Google SWE book = CC BY-NC-ND (cite, don't copy/adapt prose).

## Documentation — STRONG
- Sources: Google dev-docs style guide (CC-BY-4.0, attribution), Microsoft Writing Style Guide (CC-BY-4.0), Diataxis (doc-type taxonomy), Vale + packages (MIT engine; Google pkg CC-BY-4.0), proselint (BSD)/markdownlint (MIT)/alex (MIT), PEP257/TSDoc, Keep a Changelog, CodeRabbit docstring-coverage model, Danger API-without-docs.
- Dimensions (two halves): (A) prose/docs quality — Diataxis type-fit, voice/grammar, mechanics/consistency, clarity, inclusive language, structure, examples, accessibility, correctness/freshness; (B) code-is-documented — docstring presence/completeness on public symbols, format conformance, public-API doc coverage, README adequacy, changelog entry, comment quality.
- Triggers: *.md/*.rst/docs/, README*/CHANGELOG*, docstring-heavy diffs, net-new exported symbols; HIGH-VALUE: public/exported symbol changed WITHOUT doc update (AST detect + no doc file touched).
- LICENSE FLAG: watch CC-BY-SA on some Vale style packages (share-alike). Google/MS = CC-BY (attribution, NOT copyleft). Ship an ATTRIBUTIONS note for CC-BY sources.

## Performance — STRONG sources, WEAK triggers (ESCALATION-DEPENDENT)
- Sources: Sentry performance-issue detector taxonomy (precise thresholds: N+1 DB/API, slow query >500ms, large payload, main-thread I/O, render-blocking) = concepts/facts; perflint (MIT)/Ruff PERF, Clippy perf (MIT/Apache), SpotBugs PERFORMANCE (LGPL tool; taxonomy reference), PMD optimization (BSD), Google eng-practices complexity/over-engineering (CC-BY-3.0), eslint-plugin-react-perf (MIT), Core Web Vitals.
- Dimensions: algorithmic/complexity regressions; data-access/IO amplification (N+1, chatty network, slow/unbounded query, blocking-IO-on-async) [HIGHEST signal]; allocations/copies; inefficient data structures; caching/memoization; unbounded growth/memory [high impact]; concurrency appropriateness; frontend render budgets.
- Triggers: DB/ORM/query-builder paths; loop-containing-a-call (AST = N+1); async/threadpool introductions; serialization/large-data; CURATED hot-path allowlist; frontend files.
- FLAG (TRIGGERS WEAK): perf triggers are inherently semantic — a glob tells you a file touches the DB, not that the change introduced an N+1; big-O regressions, missing memoization, unbounded growth, premature concurrency have NO reliable file/AST signature. Two-tier: deterministic triggers = recall net for the high-signal subset (N+1, blocking-IO, large-payload, render-block); rely on BASE-ESCALATION for the semantic dimensions. This overlay depends on the escalation path more than any other.
- LICENSE FLAG: Semgrep-maintained registry rules = Semgrep Rules License v1.0 (non-competing/non-SaaS) -> do NOT vendor; use perflint/Clippy/PMD.

## LLM prompts / skills — STRONG criteria, PRIMARY SOURCES PROPRIETARY (paraphrase-only)
- Sources: Anthropic prompting + skill-authoring best practices (incl. official Skill checklist) = PROPRIETARY (paraphrase only); OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 (LLM01) + Prompt-Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet = CC-BY-SA (attribution + SHARE-ALIKE); permissive tool taxonomies: promptfoo (MIT) red-team plugins, garak (Apache-2.0) probes, DSPy signatures (MIT), Inspect AI (MIT).
- Dimensions (two halves): (A) prompt/skill quality — clarity, structure/delimiting, role framing, examples/few-shot, output-format/schema, grounding/hallucination control, context-justification/conciseness, skill discovery metadata, appropriate degrees of freedom, consistency/durability, workflows/feedback loops, tool/function-def quality, eval-spec quality, calibrated emphasis (avoid over-prompting); (B) safety/injection — instruction hierarchy, segregate untrusted content, least-privilege tool scoping, HITL for high-impact, system-prompt/secret hygiene, output validation, obfuscation resistance, jailbreak/override resistance, refusal/abstention, access-control awareness (RBAC/BOLA/BFLA), adversarial test coverage.
- Triggers: *.prompt/prompts/, SKILL.md/.claude/skills//.claude/agents//*.agent.md, .cursor/rules/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/copilot-instructions, reviewers//system_prompt constants, tool-def schemas, eval specs (*.eval.*, promptfooconfig, Inspect tasks, DSPy signatures); content signals (SYSTEM_PROMPT constants, name+description frontmatter, function-schema markers).
- LICENSE FLAG: BEST sources (Anthropic/OpenAI docs) PROPRIETARY -> paraphrase, never copy. OWASP CC-BY-SA (copyleft) -> paraphrase to avoid share-alike propagation. Permissive tool taxonomies (promptfoo/garak/DSPy/Inspect) usable with notice. Author the rubric in our own words.

## Round-1 flags summary
- NONE blocked for lack of source. Per-overlay caveats: (1) Performance = weak deterministic triggers, escalation-dependent. (2) LLM-prompts/skills = strongest sources proprietary (paraphrase) + OWASP CC-BY-SA copyleft. (3) Infra = avoid CIS (NC/SA); use Apache scanners. (4) Testing = avoid tsDetect GPL code (taxonomy free); Google SWE = ND (cite). (5) Docs = watch CC-BY-SA Vale packages.
- Recurring meta-pattern across overlays: scanner/lint-as-ground-truth + LLM-as-judge. Where a permissive scanner exists, run it as a deterministic pre-pass: infra->Checkov/Trivy, docs->Vale/markdownlint, testing->coverage/Danger. Reserve LLM judgment for the semantic dimensions linters can't score.
